meta.subject: Physics
meta.chapter: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter

derivations.title: de Broglie Wavelength of an Accelerated Electron
derivations.priority_reason: The only rigorous mathematical derivation in this chapter. Appears frequently in 3-mark questions or numericals.
derivations.diagram_description: None required. A simple block diagram of an electron gun accelerating an electron can be drawn but is not standard.

given_assumptions: Accelerated from rest through a potential difference $V$.

steps.text: Work done by electric field on the electron equals gain in kinetic energy $K$.
Relation between Kinetic Energy $K$ and Momentum $p$ ($K = p^2/2m$):
According to de Broglie hypothesis, wavelength $\lambda = h/p$.

final_result.text: The wavelength of an electron accelerated by V volts is:

examiner_notes: Students must know the value 12.27 Angstrom / sqrt(V).
Don't forget the root over V.
question_forms: Derive an expression for the de Broglie wavelength associated with an electron accelerated through a potential V.
Show that the wavelength of electrons accelerated through V volts is given by 1.227/sqrt(V) nm.

derivations.title: Einstein's Photoelectric Equation
derivations.priority_reason: The foundational equation of the chapter. Often asked to 'establish' or 'state and explain'.
derivations.diagram_description: Metal surface showing free electrons. Incident photon of energy h-nu striking an electron. Electron emerging with Max Kinetic Energy. Energy level diagram showing Work Function (Phi-0) depth.

given_assumptions: Radiation of frequency $\nu$ incident on metal.
Energy conservation applies to photon-electron collision.

steps.text: Energy of incident photon is $E = h\nu$.
This energy is used in two parts: 1. Overcoming the work function $\Phi_0$ to escape. 2. Imparting kinetic energy to the electron.
In terms of Stopping Potential $V_0$ ($K_{max} = eV_0$):

examiner_notes: Must define terms: Work Function, Threshold Frequency.
Crucial to mention this applies to the *maximum* kinetic energy, not all electrons.
Graph of Kmax vs Frequency is a straight line with slope h.
question_forms: Using conservation of energy, derive the relation between stopping potential and frequency of incident radiation.
